title: Server Configuration, HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, TLS, and Graceful Shutdown · Go Semantics Case: Protocols Http1 Http2 H2c
summary: A public server uses package defaults, has zero timeouts, trusts unlimited headers, enables cleartext HTTP/2 accidentally, serves weak TLS, and terminates active requests on deploy. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo protocols http1 http2 h2c increment in explicit http.Server protocol and shutdown configuration. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Configure Server.Protocols for HTTP/1 and TLS HTTP/2, treat UnencryptedHTTP2 as explicit h2c authority, and name HTTP/3 as outside net/http server support.
Configure ReadHeaderTimeout, body strategy, WriteTimeout, IdleTimeout, MaxHeaderBytes, and per-handler deadlines from threat and workload evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Protocols Http1 Http2 H2c · Theory Protocols Http1 Http2 H2c
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 9563 in a signed fulfillment webhook gateway under replay attack. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2 service by timeout and graceful-drain policy while excluding HTTP/3; then change the tenant and show deny-by-default before repository access. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Configure Server.Protocols for HTTP/1 and TLS HTTP/2, treat UnencryptedHTTP2 as explicit h2c authority, and name HTTP/3 as outside net/http server support.
